definition: baking is the activity of making bread, cakes, pies etc that you cook in an oven
example: I did some baking for my sister’s birthday party
bake the loaf for 40 minutes
my dad’s a really good baker

definition: a block is a group of buildings in a town or city with streets on all sides
example: we took a walk around the block

definition: a card or a playing card is a small piece of cardboard that has numbers or pictures on it, used for playing games. A pack of cards usually has 52 cards in it. If you play cards, you play a game using a pack of cards
example: I don’t enjoy card games

definition: if you end up doing something, you eventually do it, although you probably did not want to or mean to do it
example: I usually end up telling everybody everything
translation: кончать тем, что; в итоге оказываться

definition: something that is impressive is very good and makes you admire it
example: his piano playing was really impressive
what impressed me about the film was the quality of the acting

definition: a play is a story that is performed by actors who speak the words of the people who are in the story and who behave as if they are the people
example: we went to the theatre and saw an amazing play

definition: if you go sailing, you go on the water in a small boat that has sails, for pleasure or as a sport
example: I went sailing on a big lake near my house

definition: if you trust someone, you think they are honest, and you believe they will not try to do anything bad to you
example: most people seem to trust me
it’s a slow process to gain his trust

definition: your balance is your ability to stand still and not fall over. If you are off balance, your body is leaning on one side and you may fall
example: you need good balance for judo

definition: if you bang something, you hit it. If you bang a part of your body, you accidentally hit it against something
example: I fell off my bike and banged my head
the door suddenly flew open and I got a bang on the head

definition: if you bet, you risk money on the result of a game, race, or other activity. If you say to someone “I bet I can” do something, you are telling them that you think you can do it, even if they think you can’t
example: I bet I could throw you over
a £5 bet
my father always warned me against betting

definition: if you boast, you tell other people that you are very good at something or that you have very nice or expensive things in a way that is very annoying
example: Rika never boasts about her success

definition: if someone bullies you, they hurt you or laugh at you because you are different from them and weaker than them
example: the big kids used to bully me at school
he’s a horrible bully!
bullying is a big problem in the school

definition: a champion is the person who has won a sports competition
example: she’s a famous judo champion
she won the school table tennis championship

definition: confidence is your belief in your ability to do something well
example: I lost a lot of confidence after he beat me
he entered the tournament confident that he could win it
we confidently believed that we could succeed where others failed

definition: to defend someone or something means to try and protect them by fighting against someone who is attacking them
example: I wanted to learn judo so I could defend myself
my grandfather died during the defence of Stalingrad
I decided to join a self-defence class (where people learn how to defend themselves if they are attacked)

definition: if you fall off something that you are standing or sitting on, you accidentally drop to the ground from there
example: I fell off my bike and hurt myself

definition: the quarter-finals are the four matches in a competition which are played when there are eight people or teams left in the competition. The two semi-finals are the matches that are played when there are only four people or teams left, and the final is the game or match between the last two players or teams. The winner of the final is the winner of the whole competition
example: she won the final of the women’s national judo championship
she was a semi-finalist in the national championship
she was a semi-finalist in the national championship
the crowd cheered as the two finalists came onto the pitch

definition: if you go on to do something, you do it after you have done something else
example: the girl who beat me went on to win the whole competition

definition: a helmet is a hard hat that you wear to protect your head
example: luckily, I was wearing my helmet

definition: if you have an injury, a part of your body has been hurt and needs time to recover
example: I got a really bad knee injury
he injured his shoulder playing football
he injured his shoulder playing football

definition: patience is the ability to do something that takes a long time without losing interest or becoming angry or upset
example: I’m beginning to lose my patience
thanks for waiting, you’ve been very patient
they waited patiently until the bus arrived

definition: if you persuade someone to do something, you talk to them and give reasons so that they agree to do it
example: I tried to persuade him to change his mind
She has the power of persuasion to make people do whatever she wants

definition: if you do something properly you do it correctly. If something happens properly, it happens in the way it is meant to happen
example: my injury has never properly healed
make sure you get proper medical treatment

definition: if you pull a muscle, you injure it by stretching it too much
example: I pulled a muscle in my leg

definition: if you recover a mental or physical state, you get it back again after you have spent some time without it
example: it took a while to recover my confidence

definition: if you slip, your foot moves accidentally and you lose your balance or fall. You usually slip when the ground is wet
example: it’s a bit muddy – be careful you don’t slip
the river bank is very slippery

definition: if something is stiff, it is difficult to move. If your muscles are stiff, they hurt because you have used them too much
example: my legs are really stiff after the long walk yesterday
he marched stiffly to the door and went out
some stiffness is to be expected after that sort of exercise

definition: a tournament is a competition with several rounds. When a team or player loses, they leave the competition. At the end, just two teams or players are left and they play the final to decide who is the winner of the whole
example: tournament Rika won the judo tournament

definition: training is doing exercises and practice in order to become fit and to develop the skills needed for a sport
example: I had an injury which stopped me doing any training
he trains hard every day

definition: when you warm up, you move your body gently so that you are ready to start doing exercise or a sport
example: you need to warm up properly before you start your run
I always do a ten-minute warm-up before a match

definition: a challenge is something difficult which you want to try and do
example: we want to set our readers a challenge
it’s a very challenging job

definition: Commerce is business. If something is commercial, it is related to business or making money. When you say music or a film is commercial, you’re suggesting that it isn’t very good because it was only made in order to make money
example: their first album was good, but this new one is very commercial

definition: something or someone that is energetic does things in a very lively and enthusiastic way
example: the band were loud and energetic on stage
the children were playing energetically in the garden

definition: a funeral is the ceremony that takes place when someone who has died is put into the ground, or when their body is burned
example: I’d like this music to be played at my funeral

definition: a generation is all the people in a country of the same age. A generation is also a period of time of about 30 years, usually thought of as the time it takes for a family to have children and for the children to grow up and become adults. If you say something has been true for generations, you mean it has been true for a long time
example: the younger generation are keen to protect the environment

definition: if you jump, you move very quickly or suddenly, for example because you are surprised or you want to start doing something quickly
example: you made me jump! (you frightened me)

definition: a live concert or other performance is one that is happening as you are watching it, not a recording. If you see a performer live, you are present at the performance
example: I saw Madonna live last year
a live TV show

definition: if something matches something else, the two things go well together because there are similarities
example: those trousers don’t match your jacket

definition: to remind someone about something means to make them remember it. Be careful – you cannot use remember with this meaning someone or something
example: can you remind me to phone home?
we’ll send you a reminder a month before your subscription runs out

definition: something that is repetitive happens again and again, often in a very boring way
example: working in the factory involved doing a series of repetitive tasks
my music practice involves repeating the same exercise several times

definition: if you set someone a challenge or something similar, you give it to them to do
example: we want to set our readers a challenge

definition: if you sing along, or sing along to something, you join in by singing at the same time as other people
example: it’s a song you always want to sing along to

definition: soft sounds are quiet, gentle, and relaxing
example: her teacher spoke in a very soft voice
she spoke so softly I could hardly hear her

definition: if something suits a person or a situation, it feels right for them. If a piece of clothing suits you, it makes you look good
example: music to suit every mood and occasion
the classes are suitable for complete beginners

definition: an agreement is an official arrangement in which two or more people, organisations, or countries decide what should happen
example: an agreement was signed by many countries

definition: a cave is a large natural hole in the side of a cliff or mountain
example: tourists come to see the cave paintings

definition: when an area floods, or if something floods it, it fills with water from a river or lake, usually because there has been a lot of rain
example: the lake was going to flood the temple
the church was damaged by a flood in 1873

definition: a country’s heritage is its cultural traditions that have developed over a long time, its important and historical buildings, and its history
example: a World Heritage Site

definition: if something is listed, it is added to a list. If a building or place is listed, it is added to a list of buildings or places that are of historical interest and that need to be protected
example: the council decided not to list the old market building
she lives in a listed building in the middle of Stratford
